phase_name: ad-hoc UI bug fix — dual lightweight-charts rendering

completed_tasks.name: Add useMemo stable refs in dashboard/page.tsx to prevent ?? [] from creating new array refs on every render
Add data-length guards (bars.length === 0, points.length === 0) to prevent chart creation before data arrives
Switch to getBoundingClientRect() for initial sizing in Chart.tsx and EquityCurve.tsx to survive chart.remove() clearing inline styles during StrictMode double-mount
Switch ResizeObserver to use contentRect.width/height instead of re-reading clientWidth/clientHeight
Remove debug diagnostic label (bars:390) from dashboard/page.tsx

remaining_tasks.name: Commit the getBoundingClientRect fix (Chart.tsx, EquityCurve.tsx, dashboard/page.tsx)
User browser-test: refresh dashboard and confirm both candlestick chart AND equity curve render reliably

human_actions_pending.action: Refresh http://localhost:3000/dashboard and confirm both candlestick chart AND equity curve are visible after the getBoundingClientRect fix
human_actions_pending.context: The previous fix (a250804) made rendering non-deterministic — sometimes both showed, usually only equity curve. getBoundingClientRect() was applied to fix chart.remove() clearing inline styles. Needs browser verification before declaring the bug fixed.

decisions.decision: Use getBoundingClientRect() instead of clientHeight for initial chart sizing
decisions.rationale: React StrictMode double-mount: effect fires (createChart), cleanup fires (chart.remove() clears lightweight-charts inline container styles), effect fires again. On the second createChart() call, clientHeight=0 because the inline styles are gone. getBoundingClientRect() forces a synchronous layout flush and returns live dimensions regardless of inline style state.

decisions.decision: Use contentRect.width/height in ResizeObserver callback instead of re-reading clientWidth/clientHeight
decisions.rationale: contentRect is computed fresh by the browser's resize algorithm; clientWidth/clientHeight can be stale in edge cases.

decisions.decision: Use useMemo for stable empty-array fallbacks in dashboard/page.tsx
decisions.rationale: data ?? [] in JSX creates a new array object on every parent render, causing lightweight-charts effects to fire and destroy/recreate the canvas even when data hasn't changed.

next_action: Commit Chart.tsx + EquityCurve.tsx + dashboard/page.tsx, then have user refresh /dashboard to verify both charts render
context_notes: The root cause is React StrictMode double-mount interacting with lightweight-charts v5 chart.remove() which clears inline container styles. The fix is getBoundingClientRect() for initial sizing (forces synchronous layout flush) and contentRect in ResizeObserver. Pre-existing uncommitted changes in optimizations/page.tsx, backtests.py, optimizations.py, test_health.py, scripts/run_opt.py are NOT part of this fix.
